conversation_quality.criteria: Evaluate the accuracy of redundancy detection analysis, including correct identification of redundant tables, views, macros, and stored procedures across databases, and appropriate consolidation recommendations.
evaluation_steps: Verify the agent correctly identifies redundant objects by comparing schemas, DDL, and data content across databases.
Check that the agent distinguishes between truly redundant objects and intentional copies (e.g., dev/prod mirrors).
Assess whether consolidation recommendations account for downstream dependencies.
Confirm the agent explains the storage and maintenance cost of identified redundancies.

task_completion.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent identifies all redundant objects, quantifies wasted storage, and produces a consolidation plan with DROP or migration SQL.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent queried DBC.TablesV, DBC.ColumnsV, and related views to compare objects across databases.
Verify the agent produced a categorized inventory of redundant objects (tables, views, macros, procedures).
Confirm the agent generated DROP statements or migration SQL with dependency-safe ordering.
Assess whether the agent estimated storage savings from removing redundant objects.

trajectory_efficiency.criteria: Evaluate whether the agent efficiently compares objects across databases without redundant schema queries or unnecessary data-level comparisons.
evaluation_steps: Check that the agent used schema-level comparison (column names, types, DDL) before resorting to data-level comparison.
Verify the agent did not query the same DBC metadata views repeatedly for overlapping databases.
Assess whether the agent batched cross-database comparisons rather than comparing one pair at a time.
Confirm the agent skipped obviously non-redundant objects early in the analysis.
